title: Principal Specialist Customer Engineer, Platform & AI/ML
company_focus:
Analytics platform with AI/ML capabilities for enterprise
data processing
description:
We’re looking for problem solvers, innovators, and dreamers
who are searching for anything but business as usual. Like
us, you’re a high performer who’s an expert at your craft,
constantly challenging the status quo. You value inclusivity
and want to join a culture that empowers you to show up as
your authentic self. You know that success hinges on
commitment, that our differences make us stronger, and that
the finish line is always sweeter when the whole team
crosses together.

Alteryx is searching for a Principal Customer Specialist,
Platform & AI/ML. This position is remote-friendly.

Why work for just any analytics company? At Alteryx, Inc.,
we are explorers, dreamers and innovators. We’re on a
journey to build the best analytics platform in the world,
but we can’t do it without people like you, leading the way.
Forget the stereotypical tech companies of the past. Embrace
the unconventional, exercise your imagination and help alter
the future with Alteryx.

Primary Responsibilities:

- Strategic Architectural Leadership: Strategically architect
  complex, modern-stack AI-enabled analytics solutions,
  providing the technical thought leadership required to
  align global enterprise needs with C-suite business
  objectives.

- Enterprise Visionary: Act as the lead technical champion for
  Alteryx One global initiatives, utilizing advanced
  creativity and ingenuity to design resilient, AI-
  integrated enterprise frameworks.

- AI Lifecycle Orchestration: Lead the design and integration
  of end-to-end AI/ML lifecycles, specializing in the
  orchestration of Large Language Models (LLMs) within high-
  scale data pipelines and complex MCP server environments.

- Autonomous Agent Design: Spearhead cross-functional
  initiatives to design and implement autonomous agentic
  workflows that solve unstructured data challenges across
  multi-cloud ecosystems.

- Governance & Data Integrity: Govern the implementation of
  sophisticated ETL, Data Quality, and Alteryx strategies to
  ensure the delivery of high-fidelity, model-ready data for
  enterprise AI applications.

- Technical Mentorship & Policy: Serve as a mentor to senior
  and junior Sales Engineering staff, formalizing technical
  best practices and influencing product and GTM
  organizational policies regarding modern enterprise
  technologies.

Required Experience:

- Minimum of 10 years of professional functional experience,
  including at least 5 years of specific expertise in AI/ML
  solutions architecture and enterprise cloud strategy.
- Advanced LLM & Generative AI Expertise: Proven track record
  in the design and deployment of LLM-based applications,
  including but not limited to custom Retrieval-Augmented
  Generation (RAG) solutions and architectures and model
  fine-tuning within enterprise frameworks like Gemini
  Enterprise or SageMaker ML.

- Programming & API Proficiency: Expert-level skills in Python
  or Java are required for custom AI development, alongside
  a mastery of API services and orchestration tools.

- Expert-level architectural proficiency and professional
  certifications with one of the "Big Three" cloud
  ecosystems and working knowledge of the others: Google
  Cloud Platform (GCP), Amazon Web Services (AWS), and
  Microsoft Azure.

- AI Data Warehouse Specialization: Deep technical expertise
  in architecting AI solutions that leverage Cloud Data
  Warehouses (CDW) and lakehouses, with a specific
  requirement for proficiency in BigQuery ML, Databricks, or
  Snowflake integrations.

- Enterprise Security Authority: Strong command of modern
  enterprise security frameworks and regulatory requirements
  for ensuring all AI-driven analytics solutions meet
  rigorous global data governance and compliance standards.

- Experience with AI/ML frameworks and LLM-based applications
  in enterprise environments (eg; Gemini Enterprise,
  Sagemaker ML, Databricks, Anthropic, OpenAI).

- Expertise with MCP server integration and orchestration
  tools.

- Formal Education: Four-year college degree in Computer
  Science, Data Science, or a related technical field, or
  equivalent high-level industry experience.

Valued Experience:

- Advanced working knowledge of API services and integrations.

- Expert skills in ETL (Extract, Transform, Load), Data
  Quality, and Alteryx.
- Alteryx Solution ACE Certification Preferred or “black belt”
  in data preparation.

Compensation:

Alteryx is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent
compensation. Final compensation is determined by several
factors, including but not limited to relevant work
experience, education, certifications, skills, and
geographic location.

The salary range for this role in the United States is
$125,070 - $163,450 (On Target Earnings range is $180,100 -
$233,500).

In addition to base pay and commission eligibility, this
role includes clear forms of additional compensation, such
as:

- A monthly Connectivity Plus stipend of $150 to support
  remote work-related expenses
- An annual $200 home office reimbursement

Alteryx offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to
support your health, financial security, and overall well-
being, including:

- Medical, dental, and vision coverage
- 401(k) with company match
- Paid parental leave, caregiver leave, and flexible time off
- Mental health support and wellness reimbursement
- Career development and education assistance

Interested? Learn more and apply today at
alteryx.com/careers!

#LI-EM1

#LI-REMOTE

Find yourself checking a lot of these boxes but doubting
whether you should apply? At Alteryx, we support a growth
mindset for our associates through all stages of their
careers. If you meet some of the requirements and you share
our values, we encourage you to apply. As part of our
ongoing commitment to a diverse, equitable, and inclusive
workplace, we’re invested in building teams with a wide
variety of backgrounds, identities, and experiences.

Benefits & Perks:

Alteryx has amazing benefits for all Associates which can be
viewed here.

For roles in San Francisco and Los Angeles: Pursuant to the
San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the Los Angeles Fair
Chance Initiative for Hiring, Alteryx will consider for
employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction
records.

This position involves access to software/technology that is
subject to U.S. export controls. Any job offer made will be
contingent upon the applicant’s capacity to serve in
compliance with U.S. export controls.

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role directly involves designing, implementing, and
orchestrating AI/ML solutions including LLM lifecycles,
custom RAG architectures, model fine-tuning, and autonomous
agentic workflows. While customer-facing, the
responsibilities center on hands-on architectural work
building and deploying AI-enabled analytics solutions for
enterprise customers.
